If any of you 200K are looking for a place to spend your journalism-supporting budget, I know of a pretty swell, hard-hitting newsletter. 

npr.org/2024/10/28/nx-s1-516…

New from me: Less than 5 percent of DA's offices have a unit tasked with seeking out wrongful convictions. I spoke with Marissa Bluestine of the Quattrone Center about what these units do well, what they could do better, and the obstacles they face.

(Link follows)

Despite (or because of) their success, CIUs now get pushback from Republican attorneys general, police departments who don't like to see their incompetence/corruption exposed, and city officials who fear the lawsuits wrought by exonerations.

Link:

radleybalko.substack.com/p/t…

. . . building his confidence and coaxing out the magnificent beast within him. I first met Wally five years into his life, and only got to see the marvelous result -- this loyal, determined, gentle, awkward, wonderfully empathetic fellow. Truly one of the Seven Great Dogs of his time. We will miss him terribly.

We say goodbye to this handsome gentleman today. 

My wife @LilianaSegura adopted Wally 15 years ago. I don't know that any dog and human could be more perfectly suited for one another. Wally was nearly feral when Liliana got him. She spent the next several years . . .

"Just come here the right way"

Denton County and its economy are built around the University of North Texas. Denton County also went 56/43 for Trump in 2024. That's because most voters there wanted to get rid of immigrants.

Now, as international students disappear, the university is collapsing. They burned down their own house.
Trump: “They executed three young people for protesting”

“Chronic hunger” at American concentration camps reported by USA Today.
www.usatoday.com/story/news/n...

The officers who did this to her should have been arrested and charged a long time ago. 

Instead, it took this long to have her bullshit charges dropped.

The Trump administration has asked a federal court to dismiss the federal civil rights charges against the two officers who falsified information to obtain the warrant for Breonna Taylor . . . "in the interest of justice."
Well, that’s the most repulsive thing I’ve read in a minute. I suppose I appreciate Adrian’s candor in saying all of this out loud.

"In the city’s modeling scene, Mr. Zampolli also intersected with Jeffrey Epstein, the financier who would later be accused of sexually abusing hundreds of girls and young women. The men once discussed buying a modeling agency together..."

It’s really the height of arrogance and hypocrisy that the U.S. thinks it can police the election laws of other countries while our own president is openly accepts bribes to pardon people convicted of serious felonies. 

www.nytimes.com/2026/03/20/n...

One couple with a baby was held at gunpoint after federal officers slammed into their car while pursuing immigrants fleeing on foot. They then wrongly assumed the couple was part of a related protest, and blew their front door off its hinges in a SWAT raid. Bovino posted the raid on his Instagram.

Missed this when it came out a few months ago, but it's stunning reporting. Vividly captures how Trump and Miller have unleashed violence and chaos on these communities -- and how their lawless goons exact retribution on anyone who gets in their way, even unintentionally. Bovino should be in prison.

We’re quickly learning that our checks, balances, and separation of powers were always dependent good faith, and therefore incapable of preventing destruction wrought by the very sort of shameless, arrogant, mad president they were designed to contain.

The people who cancelled Jimmy Kimmel after Charlie Kirk's assassination will now control television stations that reach 80% of Americans, twice that what was legally allowed before Trump's takeover. 

The state capture of network television is almost complete.

The Trump Administration attempted to make an example of Minnesota during Operation Metro Surge.

Instead, Minnesotans set an example for the rest of the country that decency and compassion will always triumph over fear and division.

www.cbsnews.com/minnesota/ne...
I...hmm. I have follow-up questions.
All it cost them was a private jet. 🤡

This is a very important report from @davidkurtz.bsky.social, who was in court for this shocking hearing today. 

And, Judge Gallagher has, in an order, made clear what she expects tomorrow: storage.courtlistener.com/recap/gov.us...
To be fair, I believe he could have written it because it's a shitty book.

Remarkable story from Denmark's nat'l broadcaster. "When Danish soldiers were flown to Greenland in January...they brought explosives so they could destroy, among other things, the runways in Nuuk & Kangerlussuaq [to] prevent US mil aircraft from landing" www.dr.dk/nyheder/indl...
Absolute insanity - the Trump administration wants to pay nearly $1 billion of taxpayer dollars to a private French company to *not* build wind farms off the East Coast
